text: To keep in touch
To lose touch
To drop someone a line
To catch up
To reach out
To fall out of touch
To touch base
To reconnect
A catch-up
To keep someone posted
To check in on
A long-lost friend
To drift apart
To look someone up
Drop me a line when you land.
I will check in on you next week.
As soon as I am back, I will keep you posted.
If I get there early, I will call you.
As soon as I land, I will text you.
I will not leave until I hear back from you.
Once we set a date, I will book the tickets.
I cannot believe it is our last night. Promise me we will not be one of those pairs who say let us keep in touch and then lose touch.
Never. Let us make it real. As soon as I get home, I will drop you a line with all the photos.
Perfect. And if you ever travel north, look me up. You will always have a place to stay.
I would love that. Once I check my calendar, I will keep you posted about dates. Let us not drift apart this time.
Deal. I will check in on you every couple of weeks, even if it is just a quick message.
Then it is settled. We will not lose touch until we finally have a proper catch-up in person.

I want to tell you about the best lesson my travels ever taught me. For years, I was terrible at staying in touch. I would meet wonderful people on a trip, promise to keep in touch, and then quietly let the friendship drift apart. Life at home would take over, and a month later I had lost touch with someone I really liked. Then I changed one small habit. Now, before I even say goodbye, I make a concrete plan out loud. I say something like, as soon as I get home, I'll drop you a line, or if you ever come to Brazil, you'll stay with us. The promise has a hook, a real condition, so my brain remembers it. And every few weeks, I just check in on one of them. A photo, a short message, nothing grand. Since I started doing this, I almost never fall out of touch. The friends I made on the road are still in my life, because I chose to look them up on purpose.

This is a quick voice note for my friend Pricila. Hi, it's me. I'm so glad we met on that trip. Listen, I don't want us to be one of those pairs who say let's stay in touch and then vanish, so let me make this real. As soon as I finish this big project at work, I'll reach out and we'll set a proper date for a video call. If you decide to travel north next year, you have a place to stay with me, no question. And once you book anything, just keep me posted, because I would love to plan part of it together. Until then, I'll send you a photo every now and then so you don't forget my face. Take care of yourself, and let's promise each other one thing: we won't lose touch. Talk soon.
